question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: internalPaths] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to paths that are prefixes of internalPaths entries (e.g., /health/liveness?extra=data) which do NOT match the internalPaths map lookup, causing them to be logged at Info level instead of Debug, amplifying log volume and filling disk to crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set X-Forwarded-For to a private/internal IP address (e.g., 10.0.0.1) to impersonate an internal service, bypassing IP-based access controls or rate limits that are applied based on the client IP extracted by getClientIpAddress(), enabling a flood that shuts down 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of concurrent requests that each create a new tracingResponseWriter struct on the heap, and since Go's GC cannot keep up with the allocation rate, cause GC pressure that pauses all goroutines and makes 30%+ of nodes unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TracingMiddleware wraps MetricsMiddleware which wraps the router, meaning every request traverses all three middleware layers, and by sending high-frequency requests cause cumulative per-layer overhead that saturates CPU on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an X-Forwarded-For header containing a list of IPs where the first entry is a spoofed internal IP, exploiting the fact that getClientIpAddress() returns the full header value without parsing individual IPs, to bypass IP-based security controls and flood 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that contains a very long fragment or encoded sequence that expands when passed through request.URL.RequestURI(), causing the log message string to be much larger than expected and exhausting memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Write()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response path where Write() is called multiple times, but since tracingResponseWriter.data is overwritten (not appended) on each call, cause a race condition in concurrent request handling that corrupts the response writer state and crashes the goroutine, affecting 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the inner handler to panic, and since TracingMiddleware has no panic recovery, the panic propagates up and crashes the goroutine, and if enough such requests are sent concurrently, crash 30%+ of node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: internalPaths] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /metrics (metricsPath) at a rate that overwhelms the Prometheus registry's internal locking, causing lock contention that blocks all metric collection goroutines and makes 30%+ of nodes unresponsive to health checks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set X-Real-IP to a binary/non-UTF8 string that causes the logrus logger to produce malformed log entries, corrupting the log file or log stream and causing log-monitoring daemons to crash, which triggers automated node restarts affecting 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a very large number of URL path segments that cause request.URL.RequestURI() to return a very long string, which is then stored in the path variable and included in the log message, causing per-request memory bloat that crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronous nature of inner.ServeHTTP() inside TracingMiddleware to perform a slowloris attack by sending requests that take a very long time to complete, holding goroutines open and exhausting the goroutine pool, making 30%+ of nodes unable to process new requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set X-Forwarded-For to a value containing CRLF sequences (\\r\\n) that are passed to log.Info() without sanitization, causing log injection that inserts fake log entries and confuses security monitoring, allowing a real attack on 30%+ of nodes to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that is exactly /health/liveness, /health/readiness, or /metrics to suppress Info-level logging, and simultaneously flood those endpoints to exhaust the health check handler's resources, causing Kubernetes liveness probes to fail and restart 30%+ of pods?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newTracingResponseWriter()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response where the inner handler writes a response body of several megabytes, which is stored in tracingResponseWriter.data without any size limit, and by sending many such concurrent requests exhaust available memory and crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted path that contains the string representation of internalPaths keys with extra whitespace or encoding (e.g., /health/liveness%20) to bypass the internalPaths map check, causing all such requests to be logged at Info level and flooding the log system to crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user omit both X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For headers, causing getClientIpAddress() to fall back to net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr), and if RemoteAddr is malformed (e.g., due to a proxy misconfiguration), cause net.SplitHostPort to return an empty string that is logged, potentially triggering log-parsing errors in monitoring systems that affect 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path containing thousands of query parameters that are included in request.URL.RequestURI(), causing the fmt.Sprintf call to construct a log message string of several kilobytes per request, and by sending thousands of concurrent requests exhaust memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection limits in TracingMiddleware to open thousands of simultaneous HTTP connections, each holding a goroutine in the inner.ServeHTTP() call, exhausting the Go runtime's goroutine scheduler and making 30%+ of nodes unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: internalPaths] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /health/readiness at a rate that causes the readiness health check (which connects to PostgreSQL and calls /network/list and /network/status) to be invoked continuously, exhausting database connection pools and causing readiness checks to fail, triggering Kubernetes to remove 30%+ of pods from service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set X-Real-IP to a string of maximum HTTP header size (e.g., 8KB) to cause the log message constructed by fmt.Sprintf to be very large, and by sending many such requests simultaneously cause memory exhaustion that crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the inner handler to return a 500 status code repeatedly, and since these are logged at Info level by TracingMiddleware, generate a flood of error log entries that fill the disk and crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that is a very long string (e.g., 64KB) to cause request.URL.RequestURI() to return a huge string, which is then passed to fmt.Sprintf and log.Info(), causing the logrus logger to allocate large buffers per request and exhaust memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set X-Forwarded-For to a value that mimics a trusted internal IP (e.g., the node's own IP), causing the node to treat the request as coming from itself and bypassing any self-referential rate limiting, enabling a flood that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that contains a very large number of encoded characters (e.g., %XX sequences) that expand when decoded by request.URL.RequestURI(), causing the log message to be much larger than the raw request and exhausting memory on 30%+ of nodes?
